title: Tangy Tender Middle Eastern Almond Stir-fry
description: A light and tangy Middle Eastern dish featuring tender rice, almonds, and a zesty lemon-tahini sauce for a nut-free-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Almonds, sliced or ground
ingredients.notes: For a crunchy texture, optional to make it nut-free-friendly

instructions: In a medium saucepan, rinse the rice thoroughly under cold water. Rinse again until the water runs clear.
Add the rinsed rice to the same pan with about 2 cups of water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 15-20 minutes or until all the liquid is absorbed.
While the rice cooks, mix together lemon juice, tahini paste, chopped garlic, and salt in a small bowl.
After the rice is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the garlic-tahini sauce. Let sit for 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
In a skillet or wok heated on medium-high heat (350°F), add oil and sliced onions. Sauté until golden brown and slightly softened.
Add the chopped almonds, if using, and continue sautéing until they are lightly toasted and fragrant. Season with salt if needed.
Combine the cooked rice with the sautéed vegetables and nuts, stirring well to coat all ingredients evenly.
Sprinkle fresh parsley on top for a pop of color and garnish. Serve immediately while hot.
